requirement.original: allow允许一切，甚至允许彻底删除服务器：The bash safety hook (pretool-bash-safety.sh) hard-blocks systemctl restart for all production services — the /allow grant only bypasses the orchestrator gate, not this hook. These are separate. You need to run this in your terminal: sudo systemctl restart serve-trip.service
requirement.clarified: Two distinct fixes: (1) pretool-bash-safety.sh must honour the /allow break-glass for non-happy-daemon systemctl restart operations (defense-in-depth second callsite inside the non-happy systemctl block); (2) pretool-git-privilege-guard.py _evaluate_push() must call _check_git_allowlist() before its default-deny, matching the pattern already present in all five sibling evaluator functions.
requirement.what: Add /allow bypass to non-happy systemctl block in pretool-bash-safety.sh; add _check_git_allowlist() to _evaluate_push() in pretool-git-privilege-guard.py
requirement.why: The /allow command is the user's explicit break-glass mechanism. When the admin grants /allow, ALL hooks must honour it for the main agent. Currently the non-happy systemctl block and git push evaluator both ignore the /allow grant.

included: pretool-bash-safety.sh: add check_and_consume_allowlist callsite inside non-happy systemctl block
pretool-git-privilege-guard.py: add _check_git_allowlist() call + change _evaluate_push signature to accept data
excluded: IS_SUBAGENT firewall inside check_and_consume_allowlist() — no change
force-push remains blocked (forbidden-ref-mutation check stays before allowlist check)
DEV_SYSTEMD whitelist — remains empty; bypass is /allow-only

success_criteria: systemctl restart serve-trip.service proceeds when main agent has active /allow grant
git push proceeds when main agent has active /allow grant (normal push only)
git push --force still blocked even with /allow active
Both blocks still fire normally (exit 2) when no /allow grant is present
Subagents remain unable to bypass either block
constraints: IS_SUBAGENT firewall in check_and_consume_allowlist() must remain intact
inline-env injection check in _evaluate_push() must remain BEFORE _check_git_allowlist()
No changes to any hook file other than the two specified

root_cause_analysis.symptom: systemctl restart serve-trip.service is blocked by pretool-bash-safety.sh and git push is blocked by pretool-git-privilege-guard.py even when /allow grant is active
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: M1: The non-happy systemctl block at lines 802-810 of pretool-bash-safety.sh lacks a check_and_consume_allowlist() callsite. Although the global short-circuit at line 615 IS positioned before this block (and should fire for main-agent calls), the user reports the block persists. Defense-in-depth second callsite inside the block ensures bypass even if the upstream short-circuit is skipped (e.g., subagent context where IS_SUBAGENT=1 prevents line 615 from matching). M2: _evaluate_push() was intentionally excluded from the _check_git_allowlist() pattern in task 20260517-222440; user has now overridden that exclusion.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: c6653c0 — fix(hooks): /allow + /do behavior + english-only durable prevention (partially addressed M1 by relocating short-circuit; M2 not addressed)
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: c6653c0 relocated the global /allow short-circuit to before Layer 1.A but did not add a callsite in the non-happy systemctl block. Task 20260517-222440 deliberately excluded _evaluate_push() as W2.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: User expects /allow to be a true universal bypass. Non-happy systemctl services (serve-trip, etc.) hit line 802's block which has no allowlist escape. Git push has no allowlist escape in _evaluate_push().
root_cause_analysis.timeline: c6653c0 (this session) partially fixed; user reporting persists; task 20260517-222440 explicitly excluded push
observations: pretool-bash-safety.sh line 615: check_and_consume_allowlist is called before Layer 1.A (inferred: code reading)
pretool-bash-safety.sh lines 802-810: non-happy systemctl block has no check_and_consume_allowlist callsite (inferred: code reading)
pretool-bash-safety.sh line 48: DEV_SYSTEMD='' — empty whitelist ensures check_systemctl_targets_all_dev() returns 1 for any service (inferred: code reading)
pretool-git-privilege-guard.py lines 571-595: _evaluate_push() has no _check_git_allowlist() call (inferred: code reading)
pretool-git-privilege-guard.py lines 455, 480, 599, 612, 645: all other evaluators call _check_git_allowlist() (inferred: code reading)

_evaluate_push() signature is _evaluate_push(command, sid) while all other evaluators use _evaluate_push(command, data) — signature change required to add _check_git_allowlist(command, data) (inferred: code reading)
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: incomplete (inferred only — runtime behaviour not measured via live hook invocation)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: User explicitly named pretool-bash-safety.sh and described the /allow-vs-hook gap. File was read; blocking path confirmed by code trace from line 615 through lines 802-810.

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: N/A — not a regression, this is a gap left by the prior fix

pre_existing_guards.purpose: IS_SUBAGENT firewall — prevents subagents from consuming /allow grants
Blocks inline-env injection attack (agent writes CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE=1 into command string)

setup.reason: non-UI — hook / config; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths (pipeline steps, API endpoints). Hook files execute in the Claude Code harness, not in user-visible rendering paths.

evidence.observed: The bash safety hook (pretool-bash-safety.sh) hard-blocks systemctl restart for all production services — the /allow grant only bypasses the orchestrator gate, not this hook.

measured.value: check_and_consume_allowlist called at line 615 (before Layer 1.A); non-happy systemctl block at lines 802-810 has NO check_and_consume_allowlist callsite; _evaluate_push() (lines 571-595) has NO _check_git_allowlist() call

expected.value: check_and_consume_allowlist() called inside non-happy systemctl block before exit 2; _check_git_allowlist(command, data) called in _evaluate_push() before _block_default_deny_push()

evidence.gap: M1: one missing check_and_consume_allowlist callsite in lines 802-810 (defense-in-depth). M2: one missing _check_git_allowlist() call + signature change needed in _evaluate_push().

reference_source.description: Source files read in this session; existing call patterns verified by Read tool

attempts.proposed_solution: Relocate global /allow short-circuit to before all Layer 1.A-E blocks
attempts.actual_change: check_and_consume_allowlist call moved to line 615, before Layer 1.A at line 617
attempts.outcome: User still reports blocking for serve-trip.service. Non-happy systemctl block (lines 802-810) is after line 615 but may not be reached if line 615 fires — further investigation suggests possible subagent-context edge case or user testing a scenario where line 615 does not consume.

attempts.proposed_solution: Add _check_git_allowlist() to _evaluate_commit() only; exclude _evaluate_push() as W2
attempts.outcome: git commit bypassable; git push still blocked. User overrides W2 decision.

novelty_check.rationale: M1 adds defense-in-depth second callsite INSIDE the non-happy systemctl block (rather than relying solely on the upstream short-circuit). M2 extends the _check_git_allowlist() pattern to _evaluate_push() which was previously excluded by BA policy now overridden by user.

development_approach.strategy: Two minimal surgical insertions. M1: one line added inside non-happy systemctl block. M2: three lines added to _evaluate_push() + signature change.
development_approach.validation_approach: Read the modified files to confirm (1) check_and_consume_allowlist callsite is present inside the non-happy systemctl if-block before exit 2, (2) _check_git_allowlist call is present in _evaluate_push() after the two absolute bans and before the CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE check, (3) _evaluate_push signature accepts data and derives sid = _get_session_id(data), (4) call site at _evaluate_command line 663 passes data instead of sid.
